refined_topic: Why Do Volcanoes Erupt and What Happens Inside Them?
learning_objectives: Understand how magma forms deep beneath the Earth's surface
Learn what causes a volcano to erupt
Discover the different types of volcanoes and their shapes
Explore how volcanic eruptions shape the landscape over time
key_points: The Earth's crust is made up of large plates that slowly move and sometimes collide
Magma is melted rock that forms deep underground where it is extremely hot
When pressure builds up, magma pushes through cracks to the surface as lava
Shield volcanoes have gentle slopes while stratovolcanoes are tall and steep
Volcanic ash can travel hundreds of miles and affect weather patterns
Some islands like Hawaii were formed entirely by underwater volcanoes
Scientists called volcanologists monitor volcanoes to help keep people safe
